Description

I tried to compile cupy with the rocm libraries that Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS provides in its default package sources. This did not work because the cupy build tool chain was not able to find hipblas. Listed below are all the files that are installed with `libhipblas-dev` and `libhipblas0`. I tested both, not setting `ROCM_HOME` at all and setting it to `/usr`. In both cases cupy could not find hipblas.

```
$ dpkg -L libhipblas-dev
/.
/usr
/usr/include
/usr/include/hipblas
/usr/include/hipblas/hipblas-export.h
/usr/include/hipblas/hipblas-version.h
/usr/include/hipblas/hipblas.h
/usr/lib
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/hipblas
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/hipblas/hipblas-config-version.cmake
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/hipblas/hipblas-config.cmake
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/hipblas/hipblas-targets-release.cmake
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/cmake/hipblas/hipblas-targets.cmake
/usr/share
/usr/share/doc
/usr/share/doc/libhipblas-dev
/usr/share/doc/libhipblas-dev/copyright
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libhipblas.so
/usr/share/doc/libhipblas-dev/changelog.Debian.gz
$ dpkg -L libhipblas0
/.
/usr
/usr/lib
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libhipblas.so.0.1
/usr/share
/usr/share/doc
/usr/share/doc/libhipblas0
/usr/share/doc/libhipblas0/changelog.Debian.gz
/usr/share/doc/libhipblas0/copyright
/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libhipblas.so.0

```

Description

The TestChoiceChi::test_goodness_of_fit (https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py#L830) is very sensitive to data randomness, but is failing on ROCm and passing on CUDA with its current setup. 

The test is using numpy.random to generate seeds(https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/cupy/testing/_random.py#L90), which are set into cupy random generator. The cupy random generator then generates random numbers for the test. 

The seeds for cupy random generator are same on both platforms as numpy.random is hardcoded to a seed 100 (https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/cupy/testing/_random.py#L37), but the random numbers generated by cupy random generator are different on ROCm and CUDA which caused failure only on ROCm. 

The test failed on both platforms when run more than 10 times as shown below, please propose modifications to this test to make this more robust, to test the correct functionality, but insensitive to random seed.

| #   of runs | # of   failures (V100) |  # of failures (MI250X) | %Failed (V100) | %Failed (MI250X) |
|:-----------:|:---------------:|:----------------:|:-------:|:------:|
|     10     |        0        |        1        |    0    |    10   |
|     100     |        6        |         8        |    6    |    8   |
|     1000    |        43       |        40        |   4.3   |    4   |
|    10000    |       500       |        494       |    5    |  4.94  |
|    100000   |       4819      |       4880       |  4.819  |  4.88  |


### To Reproduce

Install cupy from source on relevant platform: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/install.html#

Change test iterations to desired number here: https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py#L829
For 100 iterations, @_condition.repeat(3, 10) -> @_condition.repeat(3, 100)

Replace https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py#L836 with following two lines to skip failures and continue test for desired iterations:
import pytest_check as check
check.is_true(_hypothesis.chi_square_test(counts, expected))

Command to run the test:
CUPY_TEST_GPU_LIMIT=4  pytest -s -vvv tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py::TestChoiceChi::test_goodness_of_fit

Description

The TestChoiceChi::test_goodness_of_fit (https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py#L830) is very sensitive to data randomness, but is failing on ROCm and passing on CUDA with its current setup. 

The test is using numpy.random to generate seeds(https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/cupy/testing/_random.py#L90), which are set into cupy random generator. The cupy random generator then generates random numbers for the test. 

The seeds for cupy random generator are same on both platforms as numpy.random is hardcoded to a seed 100 (https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/cupy/testing/_random.py#L37), but the random numbers generated by cupy random generator are different on ROCm and CUDA which caused failure only on ROCm. 

The test failed on both platforms when run more than 10 times as shown below, please propose modifications to this test to make this more robust, to test the correct functionality, but insensitive to random seed.

| #   of runs | # of   failures (V100) |  # of failures (MI250X) | %Failed (V100) | %Failed (MI250X) |
|:-----------:|:---------------:|:----------------:|:-------:|:------:|
|     10     |        0        |        1        |    0    |    10   |
|     100     |        6        |         8        |    6    |    8   |
|     1000    |        43       |        40        |   4.3   |    4   |
|    10000    |       500       |        494       |    5    |  4.94  |
|    100000   |       4819      |       4880       |  4.819  |  4.88  |


### To Reproduce

Install cupy from source on relevant platform: https://docs.cupy.dev/en/stable/install.html#

Change test iterations to desired number here: https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py#L829
For 100 iterations, @_condition.repeat(3, 10) -> @_condition.repeat(3, 100)

Replace https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/main/tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py#L836 with following two lines to skip failures and continue test for desired iterations:
import pytest_check as check
check.is_true(_hypothesis.chi_square_test(counts, expected))

Command to run the test:
CUPY_TEST_GPU_LIMIT=4  pytest -s -vvv tests/cupy_tests/random_tests/test_generator.py::TestChoiceChi::test_goodness_of_fit

### Installation

Built from GitHub source
